pending.body:
finding_summary: `sync_own_dirty_if_beads_healthy()` does not treat the legacy `.beads/.br-unsynced-mutations` marker as foreign or unknown work, so the supposedly owned-only replay path can still publish someone else's pending mutations.
severity: high
affected_files:
- src/adapters/br_process.rs:148-163
- src/adapters/br_process.rs:1425-1467
- src/cli/project.rs:975-1001
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:456-474
remediation_hint: When `legacy_marker_present` is set, make the owned-only replay path fail closed unless ownership can be proven (or migrate the legacy marker into an owned record first), and add a regression test for a lone legacy marker.

finding_summary: The milestone propose-new-bead sync helpers still call the generic `sync_if_dirty_when_beads_healthy()` path, so they will flush foreign pending records if another workflow dirties `.beads/` between `create_bead`/`add_dependency` and the sync point.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:3016-3054
- src/adapters/br_process.rs:1374-1416
- src/adapters/br_process.rs:1425-1467
remediation_hint: Use `sync_own_dirty_if_beads_healthy()` for the propose-new-bead repair/create replay paths, or otherwise reject snapshots that contain records from a different adapter before flushing.

finding_summary: The new bead-health check probes `br` with a fresh default `BrAdapter::new()` instead of the live adapter/runner, so health-gated `sync_import()` and guarded flushes falsely report `BrUnavailable` when callers use an explicit `OsProcessRunner::with_binary(...)` or another non-default runner.
severity: low
affected_files:
- src/adapters/br_health.rs:18-20
- src/adapters/br_health.rs:127-131
- src/adapters/br_process.rs:1043-1059
remediation_hint: Thread availability checking through the active adapter/runner (or accept an availability probe closure from callers) rather than hardcoding a new default `BrAdapter`.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 20 -> Plan and Implement
